test.name: Researcher-profile interview maps answers to normalized profile fields
test.description: Single-turn decomposition: the user states their experience level and subscriptions in the first message, so the two-question profile interview can be answered without a follow-up. Tests that init-project maps the answers to experience_level, normalizes subscriptions to the canonical enum, and stores the verbatim narration_guidance for that level.

input.user_message: init-project: Start a new project to find the parents of Patrick Flynn, FamilySearch ID LZNY-BRF. About me: I'm a professional genealogist, and I subscribe to ancestry.com and Newspapers.

judge_context: `researcher_profile.subscriptions` should be normalized to the canonical enum: `["Ancestry", "Newspapers.com"]` (case-folded, alias-mapped from 'ancestry.com' and 'Newspapers')

`researcher_profile.narration_guidance` should be the verbatim professional-level text: 'No preambles. Do the work and report results concisely. Assume fluency with GPS, BCG standards, and standard genealogy terminology.'
Should still create both research.json and tree.gedcomx.json and seed the Patrick Flynn stub from person-read-flynn
Should not leave researcher_profile at the intermediate/none default, since the user supplied explicit answers in the message
